name: Footwear & Shoe Manufacturers
gst_rates: 5% on footwear up to ₹1,000/pair; 18% on footwear above ₹1,000/pair
specific_issues: GST rate change at ₹1,000 MRP threshold
ITC on leather and sole purchases
Job worker GST compliance
Export of footwear — LUT filing
E-way bill for footwear consignments
intro: India is the world's second largest footwear producer, with major clusters in Agra, Kanpur, and Chennai. Footwear manufacturers face a critical GST challenge — the 5%/18% rate split at the ₹1,000 MRP threshold — which requires careful invoice management and HSN classification.

name: Garment & Textile Manufacturers
gst_rates: 5% on fabrics and garments up to ₹1,000; 12% on garments above ₹1,000
specific_issues: Inverted duty structure — GST on inputs higher than output
ITC refund on inverted duty structure
Job worker compliance for embroidery and finishing
Export LUT for garment exporters
HSN classification for different fabric types
deductions: Raw material costs — yarn, fabric, dyes
Job worker payments for stitching, embroidery
Depreciation on knitting and weaving machinery
intro: India's garment and textile industry is one of the largest in the world, employing over 45 million people. Textile manufacturers face a unique GST challenge — the inverted duty structure where inputs (yarn, fabric) attract higher GST than finished garments, creating a significant ITC refund requirement.

name: Toy Manufacturers & Traders
gst_rates: 12% on most toys; 18% on electronic toys and video games
specific_issues: Import vs domestic GST differential on toys
BIS certification compliance for toy manufacturers
HSN classification for electronic vs non-electronic toys
ITC on plastic and electronic components
Export of Indian toys — PLI scheme compliance
deductions: Raw material costs — plastic, metal, fabric
intro: India's toy industry is undergoing a transformation — with 100% BIS certification requirements and PLI scheme benefits driving domestic manufacturing. Toy manufacturers now face complex GST compliance, BIS-related expenditure tracking, and export compliance requirements.

name: Furniture Manufacturers & Traders
gst_rates: 18% on most furniture; 12% on bamboo furniture
specific_issues: ITC on wood, steel, and fabric inputs
GST on job workers for carpentry and upholstery
E-way bill compliance for large furniture pieces
HSN classification for different furniture types
Installation service GST on composite supply
deductions: Machinery depreciation for cutting and finishing
Labour wages for carpenters and polishers
intro: India's furniture industry serves both domestic and export markets, with clusters in Jodhpur (wooden furniture) and Saharanpur. Furniture manufacturers deal with complex GST on composite supplies (furniture + installation), e-way bill compliance for bulky items, and ITC management on diverse inputs.

name: Steel & Iron Manufacturers
gst_rates: 18% on most steel products; 5-18% depending on form
specific_issues: Section 194Q TDS on steel purchases above ₹50 lakh
ITC on coal and power (electricity not eligible)
E-way bill for all steel consignments
Composition scheme not applicable
High-value transactions — cash limit under Section 40A(3)
deductions: Raw material — iron ore, scrap, coal
Depreciation on rolling mills and furnaces
Labour and contractor payments with TDS
intro: India's steel industry is the backbone of manufacturing. Steel manufacturers and re-rollers face significant GST compliance — all transactions are taxable at 18%, high-value purchases require 194Q TDS compliance, and the sheer volume of B2B transactions demands accurate monthly return filing.

name: Pharmaceutical Manufacturers & Distributors
gst_rates: 0% on life-saving drugs (notified); 5% on most medicines; 12% on Ayurvedic; 18% on medical devices
specific_issues: GST exemption for notified life-saving drugs — correct classification
ITC eligibility on capital goods for pharma manufacturers
Schedule H drug compliance and GST
Export of pharma — LUT filing and IGST refund
R&D expenditure — Section 35 deduction
intro: India is the world's largest generic medicine supplier, with pharmaceutical manufacturers facing complex GST compliance — nil, 5%, 12%, and 18% rates apply to different products. Pharma manufacturers must carefully classify each product, manage export LUT compliance, and claim R&D deductions.

name: Plastic Goods Manufacturers
gst_rates: 18% on most plastic goods and raw materials
specific_issues: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) compliance for single-use plastics
GST on recycled vs virgin plastic raw materials
Job worker compliance for plastic moulding
E-waste and plastic waste management compliance
ITC on plastic granules and compounds
deductions: Plastic granules and raw polymer costs
intro: India's plastics industry covers packaging, pipes, containers, and components manufacturing. Plastic manufacturers now face additional compliance — Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for plastic waste alongside standard GST and income tax obligations. The shift away from single-use plastics requires careful product classification.

name: Chemical Manufacturers & Traders
gst_rates: 18% on most industrial chemicals; 5-12% on some agricultural chemicals
specific_issues: Hazardous goods transport — DGFT and e-way bill compliance
Agrochemical GST — 18% on most, different for others
Export compliance for chemical products
Pollution compliance costs — deductibility
HSN classification for specialty vs bulk chemicals
intro: India's chemical industry is one of the most regulated — combining GST compliance, environmental compliance, DGFT licensing, and hazardous goods regulations. Chemical manufacturers need expert CA support to navigate the complex intersection of tax, export, and environmental compliance.

name: Food Processing Manufacturers
gst_rates: 0% on fresh produce; 5% on packaged food; 12-18% on processed and branded food
specific_issues: GST rate on branded vs unbranded food — critical distinction
FSSAI licence and compliance costs
Cold chain logistics GST
ITC on packaging material for food products
Inverted duty on some food inputs
deductions: Marketing and advertising for food brands
intro: India's food processing industry spans from small namkeen units to large multinational brands. Food manufacturers face a complex GST landscape — fresh produce is exempt, packaged food attracts 5%, and processed food ranges from 12-18%. The branded vs unbranded distinction is critical and frequently contested in GST notices.

name: Auto Component Manufacturers
gst_rates: 18% on most auto components; 28% + cess on some items
specific_issues: Tier-1 and tier-2 supply chain GST compliance
E-way bill for just-in-time delivery
Quality rejection and credit note management
TDS on contract manufacturing payments
GST on warranty replacement parts
deductions: Export of auto components — LUT filing
intro: India's auto component industry supplies both domestic OEMs and global markets. Tier-1 and tier-2 suppliers face complex just-in-time supply chain compliance — frequent e-way bills, credit notes for quality rejections, and warranty part management — all requiring expert GST and TDS handling.

name: Electronics Manufacturers & Assemblers
gst_rates: 12% on mobile phones; 18% on most electronics; 28% on some consumer electronics
specific_issues: PLI scheme compliance and incentive tracking
Import vs domestic component GST
BIS certification for electronics
ITC on imported components
GST on software bundled with hardware
intro: India's electronics manufacturing is growing rapidly under the PLI scheme, with mobile phone and consumer electronics production expanding. Electronics manufacturers face complex GST on imported vs domestic components, BIS certification requirements, and PLI incentive tax treatment — all requiring expert CA guidance.

name: Paper, Printing & Packaging Manufacturers
gst_rates: 12% on paper; 18% on printed materials and packaging
specific_issues: HSN classification for printed vs unprinted paper
GST on composite supply of printing + paper
ITC on imported printing machinery
e-invoicing for B2B printing businesses
Waste paper and recycled paper GST
intro: India's paper and printing industry covers everything from newsprint to high-end commercial printing and industrial packaging. Printers face a complex GST scenario — the same order can involve paper (12%), printing service (18%), and design (18%), requiring careful composite supply analysis.

name: Jewellery Manufacturers & Traders
gst_rates: 3% on gold and silver jewellery; 0.25% on rough diamonds; 1.5% on job work for gold
specific_issues: Reverse charge on job work for gold jewellery
GST on making charges — embedded vs separately charged
Valuation of gold jewellery for GST
PAN requirement for gold purchases above ₹2 lakh
TCS on sale of gold above ₹2 lakh
deductions: Gold, silver, and diamond purchase costs
Job worker payments with reverse charge GST
intro: India is the world's largest consumer of gold jewellery. Jewellers face unique GST compliance — 3% on jewellery with 1.5% reverse charge on job work, TCS on cash sales, mandatory hallmarking, and strict PAN requirements on large transactions. Both manufacturers and retailers need expert CA support.

name: Construction Contractors & Builders
gst_rates: 5% on affordable housing (no ITC); 12% on other under-construction residential; 18% on commercial construction
specific_issues: ITC restriction on construction — Section 17(5) block credit
TDS on sub-contractor payments at 1-2%
GST on advance receipt from buyers
Works contract GST — material vs service split
Project-wise accounting and GST compliance
intro: India's construction sector is the second largest employer after agriculture. Contractors and builders face complex GST — the 5%/12%/18% rate structure depends on project type, ITC is blocked on construction, and works contracts require careful material-vs-service analysis. TDS compliance on sub-contractors is another major requirement.

name: Rice Mills & Rice Traders
gst_rates: 0% on un-branded rice; 5% on branded and packaged rice
specific_issues: Branded vs unbranded rice GST distinction
ITC on milling machinery when output is exempt
Export of basmati rice — LUT and IGST refund
Mandi tax and agricultural produce compliance
Income tax on agricultural income — split with business income
intro: India is the world's largest rice exporter, with rice mills across Punjab, Haryana, and Andhra Pradesh. Rice millers face a unique tax challenge — paddy is agricultural income (tax-exempt), milling creates business income, and the branded/unbranded GST distinction determines whether 0% or 5% applies to sales.

name: Flour Mills & Atta Traders
gst_rates: 0% on flour sold loose; 5% on branded packaged atta and flour
specific_issues: GST on branded vs unbranded atta and flour
ITC on wheat purchases when output partly exempt
FSSAI licence for food grade flour
Bulk supply to bakeries — B2B invoice requirements
Agricultural income split for wheat growing millers
intro: Flour mills and atta traders are fundamental to India's food supply chain. Millers face a GST challenge — loose flour is exempt while branded packaged atta attracts 5%. This split requires careful segregation of sales, ITC apportionment, and FSSAI compliance alongside income tax filing.

name: Cold Storage & Warehouse Operators
gst_rates: 0% on cold storage for agricultural produce; 18% on cold storage for other goods
specific_issues: GST exemption for agricultural produce cold storage — strict conditions
ITC on refrigeration equipment and electricity
GST on incidental services — grading, packing, sorting
Income tax on cold storage rental income
WDRA registration for warehouse operators
intro: India's cold storage industry is critical for food security, storing potatoes, fruits, vegetables, and pharmaceuticals. Cold storage operators face a unique GST situation — storage of agricultural produce is exempt, but incidental services like grading and packing may attract 18% GST, requiring careful activity-wise billing.

name: Transport & Logistics Businesses
gst_rates: 5% (no ITC) or 12% (with ITC) on GTA services; 18% on courier; 0% on transport of agricultural goods
specific_issues: GTA reverse charge — who pays GST, transporter or recipient?
E-way bill compliance for every consignment
TDS on freight payments above ₹30,000
Fuel ITC restriction for transport businesses
Section 44AE presumptive taxation for truck owners
deductions: Fuel and diesel costs (ITC not available)
intro: India's transport and logistics sector underpins all economic activity. Transporters face unique GST complexity — the GTA reverse charge mechanism means the recipient, not the transporter, may pay GST. Section 44AE provides presumptive taxation for truck owners, but fleet operators need regular accounting and TDS compliance.

name: Hotels, Restaurants & Hospitality
gst_rates: 5% on restaurant services (no ITC); 12% on hotel accommodation up to ₹7,500; 18% on hotel above ₹7,500
specific_issues: ITC blocked on restaurant GST — 5% with no ITC
Hotel tariff determination for 12% vs 18% rate
GST on packaged food from restaurant vs canteen
Zomato/Swiggy TCS compliance
Composition scheme for restaurants below ₹1.5 crore
deductions: Food and beverage raw material costs
intro: India's hospitality sector has complex GST — restaurants pay 5% with no ITC while hotels face 12%/18% based on tariff. Food delivery through Zomato and Swiggy adds TCS compliance. Banquet halls must split room rental from food service. Expert CA guidance is essential for profitable compliance in hospitality.

name: Real Estate Developers & Builders
gst_rates: 1% on affordable housing; 5% on residential under construction; 18% on commercial
specific_issues: ITC restriction on real estate — Section 17(5)
GST on under-construction vs completed property
Joint development agreement tax implications
TDS on property purchase above ₹50 lakh (194IA)
Income tax on capital gains vs business income for developers
deductions: Land cost — not deductible, capital asset
intro: Real estate developers face India's most complex GST and income tax intersection — GST on under-construction sales, blocked ITC, joint development agreements, and the capital gains vs business income classification for land transactions. Expert CA support is essential from project inception to possession.

name: IT & Software Companies
gst_rates: 18% on all IT and software services
specific_issues: Export of IT services — LUT and zero-rating compliance
FEMA compliance for foreign currency receipts
Place of supply for software and cloud services
ESOPs — perquisite tax and capital gains on exit
SEZ benefits and compliance for IT units
deductions: ESIC and PF compliance for large teams
intro: India's IT sector exports over $200 billion in services annually. IT companies face unique tax challenges — zero-rated export GST with LUT filing, FEMA compliance for foreign receipts, ESOP taxation, and the complex place of supply rules for cloud-based services. Expert CA support optimises tax while ensuring export compliance.

name: Wholesale Traders & Distributors
gst_rates: Depends on goods — 0% to 28%
specific_issues: Section 194Q TDS on purchases above ₹50 lakh
Section 206C TCS on sales above ₹50 lakh
ITC reconciliation with suppliers' GSTR-1
GST on discount and scheme income from manufacturers
Cash purchase limits under Section 40A(3A)
deductions: Credit insurance and bad debt provisions
intro: India's wholesale distribution network is the backbone of consumer goods reaching every corner of the country. Distributors now face dual TDS/TCS compliance — Section 194Q TDS on purchases and Section 206C TCS on sales — alongside GST return reconciliation. Expert CA support is essential for clean compliance.

name: Exporters & Export Houses
gst_rates: 0% (zero-rated) on all exports; ITC refund available
specific_issues: LUT filing for GST-free export
IGST refund on exports — pending claims
Advance Authorisation Scheme compliance
Foreign currency invoice compliance
FEMA — export realisation within specified time
deductions: Freight and insurance for export shipments
Customs duty on imported raw materials (DFIA)
intro: Indian exporters benefit from zero-rated GST on all exports — but must navigate LUT filing, IGST refund claims, Advance Authorisation compliance, and FEMA requirements for foreign currency realisation. Expert CA support ensures exporters claim all entitled refunds and stay compliant with DGFT and RBI guidelines.
